---

Researchers have developed a novel blood-based epigenetic clock that measures aging through intrinsic capacity, termed the MSN clock. This innovative tool provides a more nuanced understanding of biological aging by assessing the functional status of an individual, rather than solely relying on chronological age.

This advancement is significant for the longevity and healthspan field as it shifts the focus from traditional biomarkers to a more comprehensive evaluation of health and vitality. By utilizing blood samples, the MSN clock could enhance early detection of age-related decline, allowing for timely interventions and personalized therapeutic strategies. This aligns with current trends toward precision medicine in aging research, where understanding individual biological profiles can lead to more effective longevity therapeutics.

The MSN clock represents a promising step toward integrating epigenetic insights into clinical practice. I encourage you to read the full article to explore the implications of this breakthrough in more detail.

Source: [news.google.com](https://news.google.?oc=5)
